text:
Using the Inspection
Checksheet Recording
System
textPreview: Using the Inspection | Checksheet Recording | System

text:
A practical guide to finding, recording,
reviewing, and updating inspection data.
textPreview: A practical guide to finding, recording, | reviewing, and updating inspection data.

text:
Welcome the group and explain that this session focuses on the daily operator and inspector workflow.
The screenshots are from the current ICRS interface with personal and production-sensitive details hidden.
What you will be able to do
textPreview: What you will be able to do

text: Move between the dashboard and the inspection modules.
textPreview: Move between the dashboard and the inspection modules.

text: Create a new record and recognize required fields.
textPreview: Create a new record and recognize required fields.

text: Search, verify, update, import, and avoid common mistakes.
textPreview: Search, verify, update, import, and avoid common mistakes.

text:
Set expectations: this is a practical orientation, not a detailed system-administration session.
Tell participants they will complete two short practice tasks near the end.
Use “Remember me” only on an assigned device.
textPreview: Use “Remember me” only on an assigned device.

text: Never share credentials or save them on a public/shared computer.
textPreview: Never share credentials or save them on a public/shared computer.

text:
Point to the email and password fields.
Explain that Remember me is appropriate only for a device assigned to the user and protected by company policy.
If access fails, verify the email first, then contact the assigned system administrator.

The dashboard is the safest place to return when unsure where to go.
The visible modules depend on the user's assigned access.
Use the top-right profile menu to review profile information or log out.
Use the sidebar to choose a module
textPreview: Use the sidebar to choose a module

text: Tip: select Dashboard whenever you want to restart your navigation.
textPreview: Tip: select Dashboard whenever you want to restart your navigation.

text:
Read the sidebar from top to bottom and connect each module to the corresponding inspection process.
Annealing and Welding expand to show additional actions when the user has permission.
Your screen follows your assigned permission
textPreview: Your screen follows your assigned permission

text: Daily inspection modules and only the actions assigned to you.
textPreview: Daily inspection modules and only the actions assigned to you.

text: May see additional menus or buttons. This is expected—not an error.
textPreview: May see additional menus or buttons. This is expected—not an error.

text: If a required action is missing, request access—do not use another person’s account.
textPreview: If a required action is missing, request access—do not use another person’s account.

text:
Permissions determine which modules and actions appear.
A missing Create, Edit, Import, Export, or Delete action normally means the account is not assigned that permission.
Never work around access rules by borrowing another account.
The same four-step workflow works across modules
textPreview: The same four-step workflow works across modules

text: Take a final look before leaving the page. Correct item code, date, machine, quantity, readings, and responsible person.
textPreview: Take a final look before leaving the page. Correct item code, date, machine, quantity, readings, and responsible person.

text:
Use this slide as the mental model for the remainder of the training.
Most modules differ in their fields, but the overall find-create-review-update pattern is consistent.
Find the correct record before creating another
textPreview: Find the correct record before creating another

text: Example record rows hidden for orientation
textPreview: Example record rows hidden for orientation

text: Use Search for a model, item, person, or code.
textPreview: Use Search for a model, item, person, or code.

text: Open the matching row to review it.
textPreview: Open the matching row to review it.

text:
Encourage users to search first to avoid duplicate records.
The available filters change by module—for example, machine number, driver model, line, type, or date.
Open and review the matching result before deciding to create a new entry.
Create a record: complete required fields first
textPreview: Create a record: complete required fields first

text: Scroll to the bottom, review the full form, then save.
textPreview: Scroll to the bottom, review the full form, then save.

text:
Demonstrate the required-field asterisk.
Explain that the visible upper section is only part of the form; users must scroll to complete readings and remarks.
Values must come from the actual inspection source, not from memory or a previous entry.
Review first; edit or delete only when appropriate
textPreview: Review first; edit or delete only when appropriate

text: Open the record and verify the details.
textPreview: Open the record and verify the details.

text: Correct an authorized mistake; do not rewrite history.
textPreview: Correct an authorized mistake; do not rewrite history.

text: Use only for an invalid or duplicate record, and confirm carefully.
textPreview: Use only for an invalid or duplicate record, and confirm carefully.

text: Actions may be hidden when your account does not have permission.
textPreview: Actions may be hidden when your account does not have permission.

text:
Show the actions column and explain the difference between viewing, editing, and deleting.
If uncertain whether a record should be deleted, stop and ask the responsible supervisor or inspector.
Read duplicate and error results before confirming.
textPreview: Read duplicate and error results before confirming.

text:
Import is never a one-click action.
Users upload a supported Excel file, preview the parsed rows, review new records, duplicates, and errors, then confirm.
If the preview looks wrong, cancel and fix the workbook rather than forcing the import.
Annealing, Temperature, and Torque use the shared pattern
textPreview: Annealing, Temperature, and Torque use the shared pattern

text: Item, supplier lot, machine, temperature, and time
textPreview: Item, supplier lot, machine, temperature, and time

text: Equipment, person in charge, AM/PM readings, and dates
textPreview: Equipment, person in charge, AM/PM readings, and dates

text: Driver details, line, screw type, process, and readings
textPreview: Driver details, line, screw type, process, and readings

text:
Do not repeat the full workflow three times; emphasize that these modules share the same list, filters, create form, details, and edit pattern.
The field names differ according to the inspection being recorded.
Magnetism adds batches and inspection checkpoints
textPreview: Magnetism adds batches and inspection checkpoints

text: One checksheet can contain multiple batches.
textPreview: One checksheet can contain multiple batches.

text:
Magnetism differs from the simple one-form modules.
Start with the checksheet header, then work inside it to create production batches and enter checkpoints.
The current training database has no Magnetism checksheets, so the slide illustrates the live create screen and the verified workflow.
Welding is driven by checksheet type and item code
textPreview: Welding is driven by checksheet type and item code

text:
The selected checksheet type and item code determine which fields and validation guidance apply.
Users should read the blue validation message before entering measurement data.
Continue below the visible area to complete samples, personnel, and remarks.
Material Monitoring organizes main lots and sub-lots
textPreview: Material Monitoring organizes main lots and sub-lots

text: Sub-lot fields appear after Material Type is selected.
textPreview: Sub-lot fields appear after Material Type is selected.

text:
Material Type controls which sub-lot fields appear.
The letter code is generated by the system and should not be manually guessed.
Verify the main lot, quantity, operator, job number, and all visible sub-lot numbers before saving.
Prevent the mistakes that create bad records
textPreview: Prevent the mistakes that create bad records

text: Create before searching for an existing record.
textPreview: Create before searching for an existing record.

text: Ignore required fields or validation messages.
textPreview: Ignore required fields or validation messages.

text: Confirm an import without reviewing duplicates and errors.
textPreview: Confirm an import without reviewing duplicates and errors.

text: Enter “0” when the instruction means blank or not recorded.
textPreview: Enter “0” when the instruction means blank or not recorded.

text: Review the saved record before moving to the next task.
textPreview: Review the saved record before moving to the next task.

text:
Slow down here; these are the habits that protect data quality.
Keep literal form semantics. A blank or not-recorded field is not automatically the same as zero.
For imports, the preview is the safety checkpoint.
Use the trainer’s sample date and model.
textPreview: Use the trainer’s sample date and model.

text: Expected result: a saved record that opens without validation errors.
textPreview: Expected result: a saved record that opens without validation errors.

text: Use a trainer-approved sample only. Do not enter a fake record into production.
textPreview: Use a trainer-approved sample only. Do not enter a fake record into production.

text:
Allow three to four minutes.
If this is the production environment, demonstrate without clicking the final save button unless the trainer has authorized a controlled sample record.
Ask participants to point out the required fields and the final review step.
Locate and review an existing record
textPreview: Locate and review an existing record

text: Verify date, code/lot, equipment, values, and responsible person.
textPreview: Verify date, code/lot, equipment, values, and responsible person.

text: Expected result: the correct record is identified and its key fields are verified.
textPreview: Expected result: the correct record is identified and its key fields are verified.

text: Do not edit the record during this exercise.
textPreview: Do not edit the record during this exercise.

text:
Allow two to three minutes.
Provide a safe reference value that exists in the training environment.
The goal is correct identification and review, not editing.
The five habits of reliable ICRS use
textPreview: The five habits of reliable ICRS use

text: Verify the record while the work is fresh.
textPreview: Verify the record while the work is fresh.

text: When unsure: stop, review, and ask the responsible supervisor or inspector.
textPreview: When unsure: stop, review, and ask the responsible supervisor or inspector.

text:
Close by asking participants to repeat the five habits.
Invite questions and identify the local contact person for access or data-quality concerns.
